[2018-05-27 05:15] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): I dont think centurion is bad against chills?
[2018-05-27 05:15] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Centurion is vigilant so it's fine
[2018-05-27 05:16] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Plus your opponent is paying 4rr per turn to disable centurion (= deal 3 'real' damage)
[2018-05-27 05:16] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Which wouldve been much worse if you were only going for walls
[2018-05-27 05:19] amalloy: it's more like, if you own centurion you can never offer a gambit
[2018-05-27 05:19] amalloy: it's so valuable for them to freeze your other stuff to kill the centurion, so you can't do it. without centurion, you can offer the opponent weak breaches to avoid overdefending
[2018-05-27 05:20] f300xen (f0x): i mean you can gambit if you don't lose cent
[2018-05-27 05:20] amalloy: centurion isn't unplayable in bite sets by any means - it's probably still good most of the time - but it's weaker not because they threaten to freeze cent but because they threaten to freeze everything else

[2018-05-27 05:23] namington: or at least, gambits that would kill cent
[2018-05-27 05:29] gormo. (Gorm): How do you handle this ludicrous set
[2018-05-27 05:32] f300xen (f0x): ignore ampo, get tia eventually, don't even touch savior
[2018-05-27 05:34] namington: yea savior and ampo are immediately out cause tia's in the set
[2018-05-27 05:34] namington: which means its centerd around probalby a low-mid econ green-focused build, may or may not grab clunks
[2018-05-27 05:35] namington: you definitely want at least CBA, if you can fit a second B for colossuses it'd be nice
[2018-05-27 05:35] f300xen (f0x): second forge is too much commitment probably
[2018-05-27 05:37] gormo. (Gorm): I actually went tia against his amporilla but wasn't able to beat because of efficiency of walking animus and probably poor play from my side
[2018-05-27 05:37] gormo. (Gorm): Are you sure apmo isn't viable with cheap third red source?
[2018-05-27 05:37] f300xen (f0x): as soon as your opponent buys mobile animus you tia
[2018-05-27 05:37] amalloy: why did you buy 3 mobile animuses?
[2018-05-27 05:38] gormo. (Gorm): Because I never saw the unit before and didn't know what to do
[2018-05-27 05:38] gormo. (Gorm): My play is bad here, don't worry about it too much 😛
[2018-05-27 05:39] supergreyman (Greyman): You probably don't need second forge if there's Ferritin Sac.
[2018-05-27 05:40] amalloy: tbh moba/ferritin/aegis may make ampo sorta playable. i think both players should go red-green and stay around 3 green while building red stuff, dumping excess green into cryos. the moba isn't such a gigantic commitment that it immediately loses to tia, especially if you've used ferritin to get a wall out at some point
[2018-05-27 05:40] f300xen (f0x): i mean econ probably isn't big enough to ever buy clunko
[2018-05-27 05:41] f300xen (f0x): unless you're floating, but then you're really an idiot
[2018-05-27 05:41] namington: floating to get out clunk
[2018-05-27 05:41] namington: you take clunk and make it clunkier
[2018-05-27 05:41] namington: uh but i dont really think aegis enables ampo TBH
[2018-05-27 05:42] amalloy: ampo really loves defense
[2018-05-27 05:42] namington: maybe a really late ampo but not something youd focus a build around
[2018-05-27 05:42] namington: well yeah, obviously, but aegis is somewhat committal defense
[2018-05-27 05:42] namington: (the committal part, not the defensive part, obviously)
[2018-05-27 05:42] f300xen (f0x): i mean fission can get it out
[2018-05-27 05:43] amalloy: i mean, you want to be floating 3 green so that you can threaten tia
[2018-05-27 05:43] namington: i suppose thats fair
[2018-05-27 05:43] amalloy: if your opponent tias, you have aegis
[2018-05-27 05:43] namington: yeah, i guess i can see it

[2018-05-27 05:50] gormo. (Gorm): had absolutely no idea what to do lol

[2018-05-27 15:16] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): Question: When you guys evaluate unit efficiency, do you factor in the tech requirements for it?
[2018-05-27 15:22] swapgo: Thats a very important factor. It affects how quickly and how often you can get an unit
[2018-05-27 15:23] swapgo: Replicase comes in pairs (normally), Gaussite can be stored, and while Behemiun is boring you wont be having more than 1 absorb without it anytime soon
[2018-05-27 15:23] p0lari (Polari): depends in what sense
[2018-05-27 15:24] p0lari (Polari): typically for quantifying how efficient whatever unit is out of game, you factor in the cost of the tech but not how difficult that particular combination is to get
[2018-05-27 15:24] p0lari (Polari): then in-game you look at what tech overall is good in the set and adjust based on how specific units fit in

[2018-05-27 17:27] drevoed: I have a wild idea. There are some very drawn out blue sets with apollo, right? Has anybody went for sniping all 10 blastforges? :D
[2018-05-27 17:28] inasyn: What are the best units to use embargo on?
[2018-05-27 17:29] drevoed: @Inasyn 
<https://www.reddit.com/r/Prismata/comments/8l8vno/what_do_you_guys_embargo/>
[2018-05-27 17:30] wilson0862 (Wilson): @Drevoed Why would you do that? O.o
[2018-05-27 17:30] mtanzer: I'm also a fan of nothing in order to learn how to play all the units well, but some people embargo stuff they don't enjoy
[2018-05-27 17:31] drevoed: There are three schools of thought. Maximize your winrate, maximize fun and maximize player one / player two fairness.
[2018-05-27 17:32] drevoed: @Wilson so in like 9 turns of sniping they will have no tech for all the blockers in the set! and since blue is valuable, it's worth more than 2 gold, so you are sniping like 8 gold worth of value each turn.
[2018-05-27 17:32] p0lari (Polari): what about maximise consistency with tournament games
[2018-05-27 17:33] amalloy: @Drevoed fortunately  for your opponent, they'll have no tech to build blockers, but they'll have 10 walls left that you didn't snipe
[2018-05-27 17:33] wilson0862 (Wilson): You probably gave up 30 damage to do that.
[2018-05-27 17:33] drevoed: No you don't, they still spend gold on building tech buildings instead of walls.
[2018-05-27 17:34] amalloy: suppose i have one blastforge, and i buy BW, anticipating your snipe
[2018-05-27 17:34] inasyn: I think it depends whether or not you can make next turn's blocking awkward
[2018-05-27 17:34] amalloy: if you snipe the B, you're effectively converting my B supply into W supply for me
[2018-05-27 17:34] inasyn: Because after snipping a few, they overtech blue
[2018-05-27 17:35] drevoed: But you will be out of ALL blue supply in 10 turns.
[2018-05-27 17:35] drevoed: those games can get really long.
[2018-05-27 17:36] p0lari (Polari): they get long because non-goose attackers keep getting sniped and absorb/soak is good
[2018-05-27 17:36] wilson0862 (Wilson): They usually get long because both sides are having their damage sniped every turn.
[2018-05-27 17:36] p0lari (Polari): if one side keeps sniping forges instead of attack, then the game doesn't get long
[2018-05-27 17:38] wilson0862 (Wilson): In the 10 turns you've been sniping B, you've taken an extra 55 damage.
[2018-05-27 17:38] drevoed: That's kinda wrong to assume all B were free.
[2018-05-27 17:39] amalloy: @Drevoed it sounds like you don't find these theoretical arguments convincing. why don't you try it in a game?
[2018-05-27 17:39] f300xen (f0x): You have 3 blastforges by default in an apollo set, the first snipe is awful
[2018-05-27 17:40] wilson0862 (Wilson): Can you elaborate on assuming "all B were free"?
[2018-05-27 17:41] p0lari (Polari): he's saying you'll have less attack because at some point you bought a replacement forge instead of an attacker
[2018-05-27 17:41] drevoed: @f0x not neccesserely. Elyot had an option recently to snipe B to deny enemy apollo. It was also a Xeno spam set, so B was always used up on those as well.

[2018-05-28 22:02] p0lari (Polari): this looks like a p2 win to me, playing further in analysis mode
[2018-05-28 22:03] p0lari (Polari): not sure what the right build is, seems like a tricky set

[2018-05-28 22:12] p0lari (Polari): while all the apollo can do is click conduits or forges
